sections.paragraph:
ζηλωτής, οῦ, ὁ [ζηλόω] ‘one who is passionately devoted’, enthusiast

—a. w. gen., one earnestly committed: to observing Mosaic law Ac 21:20 and God’s interests 22:3; to performance of noble works Tit 2:14 and to what is generally recognized as the good 1 Pt 3:13; to possession of spiritual gifts 1 Cor 14:12.

—b. as title: ὁ ζηλώτης the enthusiast, the zealot nickname for Simon, one of the Twelve, and thus distinguished from Simon Peter: prob. w. focus on his reputation for devotion to Mosaic law and Israel’s interests Lk 6:15; Ac 1:13.
